verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation — canonically intracellular and not accessible from the extracellular face at baseline. However, LYN has documented ecto-display on tumor cells (cancer ecto-kinase pool reported on AML and other cancer cell surfaces) and surface exposure in some activated/stressed immune cell contexts. This qualifies as contextual via cell_state_induced (oncogenic transformation). Not tissue_restricted_surface in the germline sense; not a lysosomal TM protein; no stable TM-partner covalent tether; baseline is inner-leaflet, so dual_localization to outer leaflet is not constitutive.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength of evidence for LYN outer-leaflet ecto-display on cancer cells is limited compared to better-characterized ecto-kinases; could arguably be inner_leaflet_anchored.
